LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KWOK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT STATES FINDINGS AND REASONS AS REFLECTED IN THE TRANSCRIPT OF THE CERTIFIED REPORTER AND THE CLERK'S .DOC (WORD) MINUTES. A WRITTEN COPY OF THE COURT'S RULING IS HAND-DELIVERED TO COUNSEL FOR EACH SIDE IN OPEN COURT. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ANGELA BRADSTREET; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR NO. 12353, EMAIL: MVISCIO@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Appearances:
Attorney, Jason Kort of the Dolan Law Firm (415-421-2800) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Deputy City Attorney, Rebecca Bers of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-4224) appears as counsel for defendant, City and County of San Francisco

Attorney, Matthew D. Barnette of the Law Offices of Carbone, Smoke, Smith, Bent & Leonard (510-267-7267) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Aaron Kwok

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court reads the ruling into the record in open court and with both sides present.

Defendant Kwok's motion for summary judgment is granted. The Court states findings and reasons as reflected in the transcript of the certified reporter and the Clerk's .doc (Word) minutes.

A written copy of the Court's ruling is hand-delivered to counsel for each side in open court. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.
